one_time_description: Prophet River First Nation Trust contribution of $5,635,310 (prior year $9,916,575) - large trust distributions treated as expenses
economic_notes: Subsequent to March 31, 2020, Prophet River First Nation invested in government business entities that acquired ownership of a ranch for $5,100,000 and land for $315,000. COVID-19 pandemic declared March 11, 2020 with unknown financial impact on the First Nation.
notes: Fiscal year is 2020 (year ended March 31, 2020). Two OGOs consolidated (Prophet River Holdings Ltd. 100%, 1050320 BC Ltd. 100%). Five equity-accounted entities: EBA Development GP Ltd. (33.33% - dissolved), Kihew-Sas GP Ltd. (20%), Dunne-E'Nee Desta GP Ltd. (100%), Tse K'Wa Heritage Society (33.33%), Six Nations Ventures LP (16.67%), plus Dunne-E'nee-Desta LP (99.99%). Large trust contribution expense of $5,635,310 is a significant recurring item. Write-down of intercompany receivable from T'saa-Dunne LP of $842,595. No schedule of remuneration included in financial statements. Total expenses on statement of operations include other expenses (amortization, loss on disposal, equity losses, write-downs) totalling $3,184,210 in addition to program expenses of $14,016,110. total_government_transfers calculated as ISC ($1,931,977) + FNHA ($685,791) + Province of BC ($160,556) + CMHC ($4,676) + FNESC ($81,502) + NENAS ($77,674) + OGC revenue ($600000) - OGC is an industry regulator payment treated as government transfer; osr calculated as total revenue minus government transfers.
